topic: A person changing a flat tire on a car.

parsed_generation.script:
[00:00-00:02] Shot 1: [approaching car on roadside, flat tire visible] - Camera: Wide, Framing: Medium, Movement: Static, Subject: single.
[00:02-00:05] Shot 2: [kneeling beside wheel, loosening lug nuts with wrench] - Camera: Eye-level, Framing: Close, Movement: Static, Subject: single.
[00:05-00:08] Shot 3: [placing spare tire on hub, tightening lug nuts, car lowered] - Camera: Eye-level, Framing: Medium, Movement: Static, Subject: single.
parsed_generation.narration: The sequence opens with a wide view of a person pulling over to the roadside, inspecting a visibly flat tire and preparing to change it. In the second shot, the camera moves in close as the person kneels beside the wheel, loosening the lug nuts with a wrench, focusing on the hands and tire to show the mechanical work. The final shot shows the spare tire being placed on the hub and the lug nuts being tightened, with the car lowered back to the road. Across the three shots, the single subject remains focused on the tire change, illustrating a concise, practical process that communicates why and how to replace a flat on the spot.

feedback:
Timestamp blocks do not match the gold standard’s 0-3, 3-6, 6-8 structure and durations. The sequence should explicitly show jack lifting the car and nuts being loosened/remvoed in a star pattern, followed by proper lug-nut tightening, to align with the gold standard. Adopt a concise, action-first phrasing and keep the three-shot progression focused on the tire-change actions without extra framing language.
